form.question: Jelaskan apa yang dimaksud dengan Software Configuration Management (SCM) dan sebutkan minimal 3 manfaat pentingnya dalam pengembangan software!
form.student_answer: Software Configuration Management (SCM) adalah disiplin dalam rekayasa perangkat lunak yang berfokus pada pengendalian dan pelacakan perubahan pada software, memastikan integritas dan keterlacakan sepanjang siklus hidup pengembangan. form.question: Explain the difference between Centralized VCS and Distributed VCS, including their characteristics, advantages, disadvantages, and give one example tool for each.
form.student_answer: A Centralized VCS (CVCS) uses a single central repository that all clients synchronize with. Operations like adding or updating files must be communicated to the central server, requiring network access. Examples include SVN and CVS. Its main disadvantage is a single point of failure. A Distributed VCS (DVCS), such as Git or Mercurial, allows every developer to have a full local copy of the repository. This enables offline work, faster operations, and better support for branching and merging. DVCS eliminates the single point of failure present in CVCS.

form.question: Jelaskan apa itu MTBF, MTTF, dan MTTR dalam reliability engineering! Tuliskan rumus availability berdasarkan metrik-metrik tersebut!
form.student_answer: MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) adalah rata-rata waktu antar kegagalan sistem, dihitung sebagai MTBF = MTTF + MTTR. MTTF (Mean Time To Failure) adalah rata-rata waktu hingga terjadi kegagalan, sedangkan MTTR (Mean Time To Repair) adalah rata-rata waktu yang dibutuhkan untuk memperbaiki sistem setelah kegagalan. Availability dihitung dengan rumus: Availability = [MTTF / (MTTF + MTTR)] x 100%. Semakin tinggi MTTF dan semakin rendah MTTR, maka availability sistem semakin tinggi.
form.rubric: [{"minScore":0,"maxScore":64,"proficiency":"Poor","criteria":"Tidak dapat menjelaskan ketiga metrik tersebut"},{"minScore":65,"maxScore":84,"proficiency":"Good","criteria":"Dapat menjelaskan minimal 2 metrik dan menulis rumus availability"},{"minScore":85,"maxScore":100,"proficiency":"Excellent","criteria":"Dapat menjelaskan ketiga metrik dengan benar beserta rumus availability dan interpretasinya"}]
form.key_answer_text: MTBF = MTTF + MTTR. MTTF adalah rata-rata waktu hingga kegagalan, MTTR adalah rata-rata waktu perbaikan. Availability = [MTTF/(MTTF + MTTR)] x 100%. Sistem dengan MTBF tinggi dan MTTR rendah memiliki availability tinggi.

actual_result.reasoning: Jawaban mahasiswa menjelaskan ketiga metrik dengan benar dan menyertakan rumus availability sesuai kunci, serta menambahkan interpretasi bahwa MTBF tinggi dan MTTR rendah meningkatkan availability. Namun, definisi MTBF sebaiknya lebih hati-hati karena secara konsep bukan sekadar "waktu antar kegagalan" melainkan rata-rata waktu operasi hingga kegagalan yang dalam kunci diringkas sebagai MTBF = MTTF + MTTR.
actual_result.feedback: Jawaban sudah sangat baik dan lengkap. Ke depannya, pastikan definisi MTBF ditulis lebih presisi agar tidak menimbulkan ambiguitas konsep, misalnya dengan menekankan hubungan MTBF dengan MTTF dan MTTR.

form.question: Jelaskan perbedaan antara Failure, Fault/Defect, dan Error/Mistake dalam konteks software testing! Berikan contoh untuk masing-masing!
form.student_answer: Failure adalah ketika software tidak dapat menjalankan fungsi yang diharapkan, misalnya aplikasi crash saat dijalankan. Fault atau defect adalah cacat dalam komponen sistem yang dapat menyebabkan failure, misalnya bug dalam kode yang mengakibatkan pembagian dengan nol. Error atau mistake adalah tindakan manusia yang menghasilkan hasil yang salah, misalnya developer salah menulis logika kondisi if-else.

test_cases.name: TDD — Vector DB — Jawaban Excellent (EN)

form.question: Explain what Test-Driven Development (TDD) is, describe its Red-Green-Refactor cycle, and explain the benefits and challenges of adopting TDD in a software project.
form.student_answer: Test-Driven Development (TDD) is a software development practice where tests are written before the actual code. The process follows the Red-Green-Refactor cycle: first, write a failing test (Red) that defines the desired behavior; second, write the minimum code needed to make the test pass (Green); third, refactor the code to improve its design while keeping tests passing (Refactor). Benefits of TDD include better code design, higher test coverage, early defect detection, and reduced debugging time. Challenges include the initial learning curve, increased upfront time investment, and difficulty applying TDD to legacy codebases or complex UI components.

retrieved_sources.content: Principles of Test-Driven Development (TDD) The TDD Cycle (Red-Green-Refactor) Red (Write a Failing Test): Start with a test that defines a small improvement or new feature. This initial test should fail as there's no existing code to make it pass. Green (Write the Minimum Code): Write the minimum amount of code to make the failing test pass. The emphasis is on simplicity and meeting the test requirements. Refactor (Improve the Code): Refactor the code without changing its behavior. The goal is to enhance the code's structure, readability, and efficiency while maintaining the passing test. Example: TDD for a function that calculates factorials. Write a failing test for input, implement basic functionality to pass, and refine for edge cases.

test_cases.name: Software Deployment — Key Answer — Strategi Deployment (ID)

form.question: Sebutkan dan jelaskan minimal 4 strategi deployment software yang berbeda beserta karakteristik utamanya!
form.student_answer: Ada beberapa strategi deployment: (1) Basic Deployment - memperbarui semua node secara bersamaan dengan versi baru, sederhana tapi berisiko downtime. (2) Rolling Deployment - memperbarui sebagian instance secara bertahap, mengurangi risiko downtime. (3) Blue/Green Deployment - menjalankan dua versi secara bersamaan (blue = lama, green = baru), memungkinkan rollback instan. (4) Canary Deployment - merilis versi baru secara bertahap ke subset pengguna kecil terlebih dahulu untuk memvalidasi sebelum full release.

Understanding Technical Debt What is Technical Debt? Technical debt is the cost of additional rework caused by taking shortcuts or choosing a simpler, less ideal solution in the short term that must be corrected later for the system to remain efficient and adaptable. Example: A development team hardcodes values instead of creating a configuration file to save time but must revisit this approach for future scalability.

test_cases.name: DevOps — Vector DB — Prinsip CI/CD (ID)

form.question: Jelaskan apa itu DevOps dan bagaimana prinsip Continuous Integration (CI) dan Continuous Delivery (CD) mendukung tujuan DevOps!
form.student_answer: DevOps adalah praktik yang menyatukan tim pengembangan (Dev) dan operasi (Ops) untuk mempercepat pengiriman software berkualitas. Continuous Integration (CI) adalah praktik di mana developer sering menggabungkan perubahan kode ke repository bersama dan divalidasi oleh automated test secara otomatis, sehingga error dapat dideteksi lebih awal. Continuous Delivery (CD) memastikan kode yang telah divalidasi dapat di-deploy ke lingkungan produksi kapan saja secara otomatis. Bersama-sama, CI/CD mendukung tujuan DevOps yaitu pengiriman lebih cepat, kualitas lebih baik, dan pengurangan bottleneck.

test_cases.session: Session 24: DevOps Toolchains and Continuous Monitoring

form.question: Describe the main components of a DevOps toolchain and give at least one example tool for each component.
form.student_answer: A DevOps toolchain consists of several key components: (1) Planning Tools - track requirements and manage workflows, e.g., Jira; (2) Version Control Tools - manage code versions, e.g., Git; (3) Build and CI/CD Tools - automate testing, integration, and deployment, e.g., Jenkins or Travis CI; (4) Containerization Tools - package applications for consistency, e.g., Docker; (5) Orchestration Tools - manage containerized applications at scale, e.g., Kubernetes; (6) Infrastructure as Code (IaC) Tools - manage infrastructure through code, e.g., Terraform or Ansible; (7) Monitoring and Logging Tools - observe system health and analyze logs, e.g., Prometheus or Grafana.

form.question: Jelaskan apa itu GDPR dan CCPA! Apa saja prinsip utama perlindungan data yang harus dipatuhi oleh software engineer berdasarkan regulasi ini?
form.student_answer: GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) adalah regulasi Uni Eropa yang berfokus pada privasi dan keamanan data pengguna, termasuk hak untuk dilupakan dan persyaratan persetujuan. CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act) mengatur praktik pengumpulan dan berbagi data, khususnya untuk perusahaan yang menangani data pribadi penduduk California. Prinsip utama yang harus diikuti: Data Minimization (hanya kumpulkan data yang diperlukan), Purpose Limitation (data hanya digunakan untuk tujuan yang dinyatakan), dan Data Security (perlindungan data dari pelanggaran).

body.key_answer: DevOps menyatukan Dev dan Ops. Tujuan: (1) Faster Delivery; (2) Improved Quality; (3) Efficiency and Reduced Bottlenecks; (4) Customer Satisfaction. Contoh: Netflix menggunakan DevOps dengan CI/CD pipeline dan deployment otomatis.

students.answer: DevOps adalah praktik yang menyatukan pengembangan software dan operasi IT untuk mempercepat pengiriman software. Tujuan utamanya: (1) Faster Delivery - mempercepat proses rilis fitur baru; (2) Improved Quality - memastikan kode robust melalui otomasi; (3) Efficiency and Reduced Bottlenecks - menghilangkan silo antara Dev dan Ops; (4) Customer Satisfaction - memenuhi kebutuhan pengguna dengan cepat. Contoh implementasi: menggunakan Jenkins untuk CI/CD pipeline yang otomatis menjalankan test dan deployment.
DevOps adalah penggabungan tim developer dan operations. Tujuannya untuk membuat software lebih cepat dan lebih baik.

body.question: Explain the three fault tolerance techniques covered in reliability engineering: Redundancy, Graceful Degradation, and Recovery Mechanisms. Provide a real-world example for each.

rubric.criteria: Can explain all 3 techniques accurately with appropriate real-world examples

students.answer: Redundancy involves having duplicate systems or components to take over in case of failure, e.g., a load-balanced server setup distributing traffic across multiple servers. Graceful Degradation ensures that even if a component fails, the system continues at reduced capacity, e.g., a streaming service reducing video quality when HD servers fail. Recovery Mechanisms include checkpointing (saving system states for rollback), failover systems (automatically switching to backup), and self-healing mechanisms, e.g., database checkpoints that allow recovery to the last stable state after a crash.
Redundancy means having backup systems. Graceful degradation means the system still works when something fails.

retrieved_sources.content: Fault Tolerance Techniques Graceful Degradation Ensures that even if a system component fails, the system continues to operate at a reduced capacity. Example: A video streaming service might reduce video quality temporarily if high-definition servers fail, maintaining the service at a lower quality.

Fault Tolerance Techniques Redundancy Involves having duplicate systems or components to take over in case of failure. Example: A load-balanced server setup where traffic is distributed across multiple servers; if one fails, others continue to serve users.

body.key_answer: Baseline adalah versi configuration item yang sudah direview dan disetujui secara formal, yang menjadi titik awal pengembangan dan hanya dapat diubah melalui prosedur change control. Jenis: (1) Functional Baseline - spesifikasi awal dari requirements analysis; (2) Design Baseline - ditetapkan setelah fase desain; (3) Product Baseline - produk akhir beserta seluruh komponen dan dokumentasi.

students.answer: Baseline dalam SCM adalah versi configuration item yang telah direview dan disetujui secara resmi, berfungsi sebagai titik awal pengembangan berikutnya. Ada 3 jenis: (1) Functional Baseline - dibuat dari spesifikasi requirements awal; (2) Design Baseline - ditetapkan setelah fase design selesai; (3) Product Baseline - merupakan produk final yang mencakup semua komponen dan dokumentasi.
Baseline adalah patokan dalam SCM. Jenisnya ada functional dan design baseline.

body.question: What are the benefits and challenges of test automation in software engineering? Give examples of popular test automation frameworks.

rubric.criteria: Cannot explain test automation benefits or challenges
Can explain benefits or challenges with some framework examples

students.answer: Benefits of test automation include increased testing speed, improved test coverage, early defect detection, and consistency since automation reduces human error. Challenges include high initial setup costs, maintenance overhead as tests must evolve with code changes, and false positives from poorly designed tests. Popular frameworks include JUnit for Java unit testing and Selenium for web browser automation and end-to-end testing.

Early Defect Detection: Automated tests can run with each code commit, catching defects early before they escalate. Consistency and Reliability: Automation reduces human error, ensuring test results remain consistent.

Benefits and Challenges of Automation Challenges of test Automation Initial Setup Costs: High setup and maintenance costs for scripts, tools, and frameworks. retrieved_sources.content: Principles of Test-Driven Development (TDD) The TDD Cycle (Red-Green-Refactor) Red (Write a Failing Test): Start with a test that defines a small improvement or new feature. This initial test should fail as there's no existing code to make it pass. Green (Write the Minimum Code): Write the minimum amount of code to make the failing test pass. The emphasis is on simplicity and meeting the test requirements. Refactor (Improve the Code): Refactor the code without changing its behavior. The goal is to enhance the code's structure, readability, and efficiency while maintaining the passing test. Example: TDD for a function that calculates factorials. Write a failing test for input, implement basic functionality to pass, and refine for edge cases.

Principles of Test-Driven Development (TDD) Key TDD Principles Small, Incremental Changes: Code is added in small, testable steps. Focus on Requirements: Tests define specific behaviors to ensure code meets requirements. Refactoring Confidence: Ensures safe refactoring without breaking functionality.
Principles of Test-Driven Development (TDD) Definition of TDD TDD is a development approach where tests are written before code, guiding the design and development process to meet specified behavior.

retrieved_sources.content: Principles of Test-Driven Development (TDD) The TDD Cycle (Red-Green-Refactor) Red (Write a Failing Test): Start with a test that defines a small improvement or new feature. This initial test should fail as there's no existing code to make it pass. Green (Write the Minimum Code): Write the minimum amount of code to make the failing test pass. The emphasis is on simplicity and meeting the test requirements. Refactor (Improve the Code): Refactor the code without changing its behavior. The goal is to enhance the code's structure, readability, and efficiency while maintaining the passing test. Example: TDD for a function that calculates factorials. Write a failing test for input, implement basic functionality to pass, and refine for edge cases.

Focus on Requirements: Tests define specific behaviors to ensure code meets requirements. Refactoring Confidence: Ensures safe refactoring without breaking functionality.
Principles of Test-Driven Development (TDD) Definition of TDD TDD is a development approach where tests are written before code, guiding the design and development process to meet specified behavior.

retrieved_sources.content: Overview of DevOps Toolchains Definition and Purpose of DevOps Toolchains Definition of a DevOps Toolchain: A DevOps toolchain is a set of tools that automate and streamline the development, testing, integration, and deployment processes. It covers the software lifecycle from coding to monitoring in production. Purpose of a DevOps Toolchain: Enhances collaboration between teams, reduces errors, accelerates deployment, and supports continuous integration and deployment.

Session 24 – DevOps Toolchains and Continuous Monitoring Content outline Understand the purpose and components of a DevOps toolchain. Explore CI and CD tools commonly used in DevOps practices. Learn about containerization and orchestration tools. Discover Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools. Understand the importance of continuous monitoring and logging. Learn best practices for building and managing an effective DevOps toolchain.

Overview of DevOps Toolchains Components of a Typical Toolchain Planning Tools: Track requirements and manage workflows (e.g., Jira). Version Control Tools: Manage code versions (e.g., Git). Build and CI/CD Tools: Automate testing, integration, and deployment (e.g., Jenkins). Monitoring and Logging Tools: Observe system health and analyze logs (e.g., Prometheus). Example: A retail company’s toolchain includes Git for version control, Jenkins for CI/CD, Docker for containerization, and Grafana for monitoring performance.
